records.question: For NYC yellow taxi trips between January 1 and January 7, 2016, excluding any trips picked up in ‘EWR’ or ‘Staten Island,’ determine the proportion of rides that fall into each tip category in each pickup borough. Only include trips where the dropoff time is after the pickup time, the passenger count is greater than zero, and trip_distance, tip_amount, tolls_amount, mta_tax, fare_amount, and total_amount are all non-negative. Classify the tip percentage as follows: 0% (no tip), up to 5%, 5% to 10%, 10% to 15%, 15% to 20%, 20% to 25%, and more than 25%.
records.pred_sql:
WITH
  trips AS (
    SELECT
      pickup_datetime,
      dropoff_datetime,
      passenger_count,
      trip_distance,
      tip_amount,
      tolls_amount,
      mta_tax,
      fare_amount,
      total_amount,
      pickup_location_id,
      dropoff_location_id
    FROM
      `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_yellow_trips_2016`
    WHERE
      pickup_datetime >= '2016-01-01'
      AND pickup_datetime < '2016-01-08'
      AND dropoff_datetime > pickup_datetime
      AND passenger_count > 0
      AND trip_distance >= 0
      AND tip_amount >= 0
      AND tolls_amount >= 0
      AND mta_tax >= 0
      AND fare_amount >= 0
      AND total_amount >= 0
  ),
  zones AS (
    SELECT
      location_id,
      borough
    FROM
      `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.taxi_zone_geom`
  ),
  valid_trips AS (
    SELECT
      t.*,
      z.borough AS pickup_borough
    FROM
      trips t
      INNER JOIN zones z ON t.pickup_location_id = z.location_id
    WHERE
      z.borough NOT IN ('EWR', 'Staten Island')
  ),
  tip_categories AS (
    SELECT
      pickup_borough,
      CASE
        WHEN tip_amount = 0 THEN 'no tip'
        WHEN SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 <= 5 THEN 'up to 5%'
        WHEN SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 <= 10 THEN '5% to 10%'
        WHEN SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 <= 15 THEN '10% to 15%'
        WHEN SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 <= 20 THEN '15% to 20%'
        WHEN SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 <= 25 THEN '20% to 25%'
        ELSE 'more than 25%'
      END AS tip_category
    FROM
      valid_trips
    WHERE
      total_amount > 0
  )
SELECT
  pickup_borough,
  tip_category,
  COUNT(*) AS trip_count,
  ROUND(COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (PARTITION BY pickup_borough), 4) AS proportion
FROM
  tip_categories
GROUP BY
  pickup_borough,
  tip_category
ORDER BY
  pickup_borough,
  tip_category

    mta_tax,

    total_amount,
    pickup_datetime,
    dropoff_datetime
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_yellow_trips_2016`
  WHERE
    pickup_datetime >= '2016-01-01'
    AND pickup_datetime < '2016-01-08'
    AND dropoff_datetime >= '2016-01-01'
    AND dropoff_datetime < '2016-01-08'
    AND dropoff_datetime > pickup_datetime
    AND passenger_count > 0
    AND trip_distance >= 0
    AND tip_amount >= 0
    AND tolls_amount >= 0
    AND mta_tax >= 0
    AND fare_amount >= 0
    AND total_amount >= 0
),
tip_calc AS (
  SELECT
    pickup_location_id,
    CASE
      WHEN total_amount = 0 THEN 0
      ELSE (tip_amount * 100) / total_amount
    END AS tip_rate
  FROM trip_data
),
tip_category AS (
  SELECT
    pickup_location_id,
    CASE WHEN tip_rate = 0 THEN 'no tip' ELSE 'has tip' END AS tip_category
  FROM tip_calc
),
borough_mapping AS (
  SELECT
    location_id AS pickup_location_id,
    borough
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.taxi_zone_geom`
)
SELECT
  b.borough,
  COUNT(*) AS total_trips,
  SUM(CASE WHEN tc.tip_category = 'no tip' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS no_tip_trips,
  ROUND(
    (SUM(CASE WHEN tc.tip_category = 'no tip' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) * 100.0) / COUNT(*),
    2
  ) AS no_tip_percentage
FROM tip_category tc
JOIN borough_mapping b
  ON tc.pickup_location_id = b.pickup_location_id
GROUP BY b.borough
ORDER BY b.borough

records.question: Find the top 10 taxi trips in New York City between July 1 and July 7, 2016 (ensuring both pickup and dropoff times fall within these dates) where the passenger count is greater than five, the trip distance is at least ten miles, and there are no negative fare-related amounts (including tip, tolls, mta tax, fare, and total costs). Exclude any trips where the dropoff time is not strictly after the pickup time, then sort the results by total fare amount in descending order. Finally, display each trip’s pickup zone, dropoff zone, trip duration in seconds, driving speed in miles per hour, and tip rate as a percentage of the total fare amount.
records.pred_sql:
WITH trips AS (
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2018`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2019`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2020`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2021`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2022`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2023`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2017`
  UNION ALL
  SELECT * FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_taxi_trips.tlc_green_trips_2014`
)
SELECT
  pickup_location_id AS pickup_zone,
  dropoff_location_id AS dropoff_zone,
  TIMESTAMP_DIFF(dropoff_datetime, pickup_datetime, SECOND) AS trip_duration_seconds,
  SAFE_DIVIDE(trip_distance, TIMESTAMP_DIFF(dropoff_datetime, pickup_datetime, SECOND) / 3600) AS driving_speed_mph,
  SAFE_DIVIDE(tip_amount, total_amount) * 100 AS tip_rate_percentage
FROM trips
WHERE
  pickup_datetime >= '2016-07-01 00:00:00'
  AND pickup_datetime < '2016-07-08 00:00:00'
  AND dropoff_datetime >= '2016-07-01 00:00:00'
  AND dropoff_datetime < '2016-07-08 00:00:00'
  AND passenger_count > 5
  AND trip_distance >= 10
  AND tip_amount >= 0
  AND tolls_amount >= 0
  AND mta_tax >= 0
  AND fare_amount >= 0
  AND total_amount >= 0
  AND dropoff_datetime > pickup_datetime
ORDER BY total_amount DESC
LIMIT 10

records.question: For each New York City borough, how many subway stations are there in total, how many have at least one entrance that is marked both as an actual entry and as ADA-compliant, and what percentage of the total stations in each borough does this represent, listing boroughs from the highest to the lowest percentage?
records.pred_sql:
WITH station_boroughs AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT s.station_name, s.borough_name
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_subway.stations` s
),
station_entry_ada AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT e.station_name, e.ada_compliant, e.entry
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.new_york_subway.station_entrances` e
  WHERE e.entry = TRUE AND e.ada_compliant = TRUE
),
borough_totals AS (
  SELECT sb.borough_name, COUNT(DISTINCT sb.station_name) AS total_stations
  FROM station_boroughs sb
  GROUP BY sb.borough_name
),
borough_ada_entry AS (
  SELECT sb.borough_name, COUNT(DISTINCT sb.station_name) AS ada_entry_stations
  FROM station_boroughs sb
  INNER JOIN station_entry_ada sea ON sb.station_name = sea.station_name
  GROUP BY sb.borough_name
)
SELECT 
  bt.borough_name,
  bt.total_stations,
  COALESCE(bae.ada_entry_stations, 0) AS ada_entry_stations,
  ROUND(COALESCE(bae.ada_entry_stations, 0) * 100.0 / bt.total_stations, 2) AS percentage
FROM borough_totals bt
LEFT JOIN borough_ada_entry bae ON bt.borough_name = bae.borough_name
ORDER BY percentage DESC

records.question: What is the total distance traveled by each bike in the San Francisco Bikeshare program, measured in meters? Use data from bikeshare trips and stations to calculate this.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  t.bike_number,
  SUM(
    111320 * SQRT(
      POW((s1.latitude - s2.latitude) * (ACOS(-1) / 180), 2) +
      POW((s1.longitude - s2.longitude) * (ACOS(-1) / 180) * COS((s1.latitude + s2.latitude) / 2 * (ACOS(-1) / 180)), 2)
    )
  ) AS total_distance_meters
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco.bikeshare_trips` t
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco.bikeshare_stations` s1
ON
  t.start_station_id = s1.station_id
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco.bikeshare_stations` s2
ON
  t.end_station_id = s2.station_id
GROUP BY
  t.bike_number
ORDER BY
  total_distance_meters DESC

records.question: Please find, for each year-month combination (in the format YYYYMM) derived from the start date of bike share trips in San Francisco, the first trip duration in minutes, the last trip duration in minutes, the highest trip duration in minutes, and the lowest trip duration in minutes, where ‘first’ and ‘last’ are determined by the chronological order of the trip start date, then group your results by this year-month and sort them by the same year-month key.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%Y%m', start_date) AS year_month,
  MIN(CASE WHEN rn_asc = 1 THEN duration_sec END) / 60 AS first_trip_duration_minutes,
  MIN(CASE WHEN rn_desc = 1 THEN duration_sec END) / 60 AS last_trip_duration_minutes,
  MAX(duration_sec) / 60 AS highest_trip_duration_minutes,
  MIN(duration_sec) / 60 AS lowest_trip_duration_minutes
FROM (
  SELECT
    start_date,
    duration_sec,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%Y%m', start_date) ORDER BY start_date ASC) AS rn_asc,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%Y%m', start_date) ORDER BY start_date DESC) AS rn_desc
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco.bikeshare_trips`
)
GROUP BY year_month
ORDER BY year_month

records.question: Find the latest ride data for each region between 2014 and 2017. I want to know the name of each region, the trip ID of this ride, the ride duration, the start time, the starting station, and the gender of the rider.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  r.name AS region_name,
  t.trip_id,
  t.duration_sec AS ride_duration_seconds,
  t.start_date AS start_time,
  t.start_station_name,
  t.member_gender
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_trips` t
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_station_info` s
  ON CAST(t.start_station_id AS STRING) = s.station_id
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_regions` r
  ON s.region_id = r.region_id
WHERE
  EXTRACT(YEAR FROM t.start_date) BETWEEN 2014 AND 2017
QUALIFY
  ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY r.region_id ORDER BY t.start_date DESC) = 1
ORDER BY
  r.name

records.question: Could you provide the details of the top 5 longest bike share trips that started between July 1, 2017, and December 31, 2017, including the trip ID, duration in seconds, start date, start station name, route (derived from start station name to end station name), bike number, subscriber type, member's birth year, the member's current age (calculated using the current year), an age classification based on whether the member is younger than 40, between 40 and 60, or older than 60, the member's gender, and the name of the region of the start station? Please exclude any trips where the start station name, member's birth year, or member's gender is not specified.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    t."trip_id",
    t."duration_sec",
    t."start_date",
    t."start_station_name",
    t."start_station_name" || ' - ' || t."end_station_name" AS route,
    t."bike_number",
    t."subscriber_type",
    t."member_birth_year",
    2025 - t."member_birth_year" AS current_age,
    CASE 
        WHEN 2025 - t."member_birth_year" < 40 THEN 'Young (<40 Y.O)'
        WHEN 2025 - t."member_birth_year" BETWEEN 40 AND 60 THEN 'Adult (40-60 Y.O)'
        ELSE 'Senior Adult (>60 Y.O)'
    END AS age_classification,
    t."member_gender",
    s."region_id" AS region_name
FROM SAN_FRANCISCO_BIKESHARE.BIKESHARE_TRIPS t
LEFT JOIN SAN_FRANCISCO_BIKESHARE.BIKESHARE_STATION_INFO s
    ON t."start_station_id" = CAST(s."station_id" AS NUMBER)
WHERE t."start_date" >= 1498867200000000  -- July 1, 2017 in microseconds
  AND t."start_date" < 1514764800000000   -- January 1, 2018 in microseconds
  AND t."start_station_name" IS NOT NULL
  AND t."member_birth_year" IS NOT NULL
  AND t."member_gender" IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY t."duration_sec" DESC
LIMIT 5

records.question: Which month in 2017 had the largest absolute difference between cumulative bike usage minutes for customers and subscribers?	Which month (in number) in 2017 had the largest absolute difference between cumulative bike usage minutes (in thousands) for customers and subscribers, based on the trip end dates in the San Francisco bikeshare data?
records.pred_sql:
WITH customer_minutes AS (
  SELECT
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM end_date) AS month,
    SUM(duration_sec) / 60.0 AS total_minutes
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_trips`
  WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM end_date) = 2017
    AND subscriber_type = 'Customer'
  GROUP BY month
),
subscriber_minutes AS (
  SELECT
    EXTRACT(MONTH FROM end_date) AS month,
    SUM(duration_sec) / 60.0 AS total_minutes
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_trips`
  WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM end_date) = 2017
    AND subscriber_type = 'Subscriber'
  GROUP BY month
)
SELECT
  c.month,
  ROUND(ABS(c.total_minutes - s.total_minutes) / 1000.0, 2) AS diff_thousands
FROM customer_minutes c
JOIN subscriber_minutes s ON c.month = s.month
ORDER BY diff_thousands DESC
LIMIT 1

records.question: For trips where 'Clay St & Drumm St' occurs before 'Sacramento St & Davis St' in the stop sequence (one direction only), what are the earliest departure times from 'Clay St & Drumm St' and the latest arrival times at 'Sacramento St & Davis St' in the format HH:MM:SS? Please provide the trip headsign for each route.
records.pred_sql:
WITH clay_stop AS (
  SELECT stop_id
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_transit_muni.stops`
  WHERE stop_name = 'Clay St & Drumm St'
),
sacramento_stop AS (
  SELECT stop_id
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_transit_muni.stops`
  WHERE stop_name = 'Sacramento St & Davis St'
),
trip_stops AS (
  SELECT 
    st.trip_id,
    st.stop_id,
    st.stop_sequence,
    st.arrival_time,
    st.departure_time
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_transit_muni.stop_times` st
  WHERE st.stop_id IN (SELECT stop_id FROM clay_stop UNION ALL SELECT stop_id FROM sacramento_stop)
),
clay_stops AS (
  SELECT trip_id, stop_sequence, departure_time
  FROM trip_stops
  WHERE stop_id = (SELECT stop_id FROM clay_stop)
),
sacramento_stops AS (
  SELECT trip_id, stop_sequence, arrival_time
  FROM trip_stops
  WHERE stop_id = (SELECT stop_id FROM sacramento_stop)
),
valid_trips AS (
  SELECT c.trip_id
  FROM clay_stops c
  JOIN sacramento_stops s ON c.trip_id = s.trip_id
  WHERE c.stop_sequence < s.stop_sequence
)
SELECT 
  t.trip_headsign,
  FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%H:%M:%S', PARSE_TIMESTAMP('%H:%M:%S', c.departure_time)) AS earliest_departure_clay,
  FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%H:%M:%S', PARSE_TIMESTAMP('%H:%M:%S', s.arrival_time)) AS latest_arrival_sacramento
FROM valid_trips vt
JOIN clay_stops c ON vt.trip_id = c.trip_id
JOIN sacramento_stops s ON vt.trip_id = s.trip_id
JOIN `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_transit_muni.trips` t ON vt.trip_id = t.trip_id
WHERE c.departure_time = (
  SELECT MIN(c2.departure_time)
  FROM clay_stops c2
  WHERE c2.trip_id = vt.trip_id
)
AND s.arrival_time = (
  SELECT MAX(s2.arrival_time)
  FROM sacramento_stops s2
  WHERE s2.trip_id = vt.trip_id
)
ORDER BY t.trip_headsign

records.question: What is the highest average speed (rounded to 1 decimal, in metric m/s) for bike trips in Berkeley with trip distance greater than 1000 meters?
records.pred_sql:
WITH berkeley_trips AS (
  SELECT 
    trip_id,
    duration_sec,
    ST_DISTANCE(
      ST_GEOGPOINT(start_station_longitude, start_station_latitude),
      ST_GEOGPOINT(end_station_longitude, end_station_latitude)
    ) AS distance_meters
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_trips`
  WHERE 
    start_station_name LIKE '%Berkeley%' 
    OR end_station_name LIKE '%Berkeley%'
),
filtered_trips AS (
  SELECT 
    trip_id,
    duration_sec,
    distance_meters
  FROM berkeley_trips
  WHERE distance_meters > 1000 AND duration_sec > 0
)
SELECT 
  ROUND(AVG(distance_meters / duration_sec), 1) AS avg_speed_mps
FROM filtered_trips

records.question: For each neighborhood in San Francisco where at least one bike share station and at least one crime incident are located, provide the neighborhood name along with the total count of bike share stations and the total number of crime incidents in that neighborhood.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  n.neighborhood,
  COUNT(DISTINCT s.station_id) AS bike_share_stations,
  COUNT(DISTINCT i.unique_key) AS crime_incidents
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_neighborhoods.boundaries` n
LEFT JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_bikeshare.bikeshare_station_info` s
ON
  ST_WITHIN(ST_GEOGPOINT(s.lon, s.lat), n.neighborhood_geom)
LEFT JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.san_francisco_sfpd_incidents.sfpd_incidents` i
ON
  ST_WITHIN(ST_GEOGPOINT(i.longitude, i.latitude), n.neighborhood_geom)
GROUP BY
  n.neighborhood
HAVING
  bike_share_stations > 0
  AND crime_incidents > 0
ORDER BY
  n.neighborhood

records.question: Can you help me figure out the revenue for the product category that has the highest number of customers making a purchase in their first non-cancelled and non-returned order?
records.pred_sql:
WITH first_non_cancelled_non_returned_orders AS (
    SELECT 
        o."user_id",
        MIN(o."order_id") AS first_order_id
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o
    WHERE o."status" NOT IN ('Cancelled', 'Returned')
      AND o."returned_at" IS NULL
    GROUP BY o."user_id"
),
first_order_products AS (
    SELECT 
        f."user_id",
        oi."product_id"
    FROM first_non_cancelled_non_returned_orders f
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi ON f.first_order_id = oi."order_id"
),
category_customer_count AS (
    SELECT 
        p."category",
        COUNT(DISTINCT fop."user_id") AS customer_count
    FROM first_order_products fop
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON fop."product_id" = p."id"
    GROUP BY p."category"
    ORDER BY customer_count DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
top_category AS (
    SELECT "category" FROM category_customer_count
)
SELECT 
    SUM(oi."sale_price") AS revenue
FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi
JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON oi."product_id" = p."id"
WHERE p."category" = (SELECT "category" FROM top_category)

records.question: Among all product categories in the dataset, identify the category with the highest total purchase quantity (based on order_items table), and for that specific category, what is the average time in minutes that users spend on each product page visit? The average time should be calculated as the difference between the timestamp when a user views a product page and the timestamp of the next event within the same session
records.pred_sql:
WITH category_purchase AS (
    SELECT 
        p."category",
        COUNT(oi."id") AS total_purchase_qty
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON oi."product_id" = p."id"
    GROUP BY p."category"
    ORDER BY total_purchase_qty DESC
    LIMIT 1
),
category_events AS (
    SELECT 
        e."user_id",
        e."session_id",
        e."created_at" AS event_time,
        LEAD(e."created_at") OVER (PARTITION BY e."session_id" ORDER BY e."created_at") AS next_event_time
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.EVENTS e
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi ON e."user_id" = oi."user_id"
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON oi."product_id" = p."id"
    WHERE p."category" = (SELECT "category" FROM category_purchase)
      AND e."event_type" = 'product'
)
SELECT 
    (SELECT "category" FROM category_purchase) AS top_category,
    AVG((next_event_time - event_time) / 60) AS avg_time_minutes
FROM category_events
WHERE next_event_time IS NOT NULL

records.question: Generate a monthly report for each product category , where each row corresponds to orders that have a status of 'Complete' and were delivered before the year 2022, grouping by the month and year of delivery. For each category, calculate the total revenue (the sum of sale_price), the total number of completed orders, and compute the month-over-month percentage growth for both revenue and orders by comparing each month’s totals to the previous month’s. Then, for the same orders, aggregate and show the total cost (from product costs), total profit (revenue minus total cost), and finally the profit-to-cost ratio for each month.
records.pred_sql:
WITH delivered_orders AS (
    SELECT 
        oi.product_id,
        oi.sale_price,
        oi.delivered_at,
        EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(oi.delivered_at / 1000000)) AS delivery_year,
        EXTRACT(MONTH FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(oi.delivered_at / 1000000)) AS delivery_month,
        TO_CHAR(TO_TIMESTAMP(oi.delivered_at / 1000000), 'YYYY-MM') AS year_month
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id
    WHERE oi.status = 'Complete'
      AND oi.delivered_at IS NOT NULL
      AND TO_TIMESTAMP(oi.delivered_at / 1000000) < '2022-01-01'
),
product_categories AS (
    SELECT 
        p.id AS product_id,
        p.category
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p
),
monthly_aggregates AS (
    SELECT 
        pc.category,
        do.delivery_year,
        do.delivery_month,
        do.year_month,
        SUM(do.sale_price) AS total_revenue,
        COUNT(*) AS total_orders,
        SUM(ii.cost) AS total_cost
    FROM delivered_orders do
    JOIN product_categories pc ON do.product_id = pc.product_id
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.INVENTORY_ITEMS ii ON do.product_id = ii.product_id
    GROUP BY pc.category, do.delivery_year, do.delivery_month, do.year_month
),
with_lag AS (
    SELECT 
        category,
        delivery_year,
        delivery_month,
        year_month,
        total_revenue,
        total_orders,
        total_cost,
        LAG(total_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY delivery_year, delivery_month) AS prev_revenue,
        LAG(total_orders) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY delivery_year, delivery_month) AS prev_orders
    FROM monthly_aggregates
)
SELECT 
    category,
    delivery_year,
    delivery_month,
    year_month,
    total_revenue,
    total_orders,
    ROUND((total_revenue - prev_revenue) / NULLIF(prev_revenue, 0) * 100, 2) AS revenue_growth_pct,
    ROUND((total_orders - prev_orders) / NULLIF(prev_orders, 0) * 100, 2) AS orders_growth_pct,
    total_cost,
    (total_revenue - total_cost) AS total_profit,
    ROUND((total_revenue - total_cost) / NULLIF(total_cost, 0), 4) AS profit_to_cost_ratio
FROM with_lag
ORDER BY category, delivery_year, delivery_month

records.question: Using data up to the end of 2022 and organized by the month of each user's first purchase, can you provide the percentage of users who made a purchase in each of the first, second, third, and fourth months since their initial purchase, where the "first month" refers to the month of their initial purchase?
records.pred_sql:
WITH first_purchase AS (
    SELECT 
        u."id" AS user_id,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(MIN(o."created_at"))) AS first_purchase_month
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.USERS u
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o ON u."id" = o."user_id"
    WHERE o."created_at" <= 1672531199  -- end of 2022 (unix timestamp for 2022-12-31 23:59:59)
    GROUP BY u."id"
),
user_months AS (
    SELECT 
        fp.user_id,
        fp.first_purchase_month,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(o."created_at")) AS purchase_month,
        DATEDIFF('month', fp.first_purchase_month, DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(o."created_at"))) AS month_offset
    FROM first_purchase fp
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o ON fp.user_id = o."user_id"
    WHERE o."created_at" <= 1672531199
),
monthly_cohort AS (
    SELECT 
        first_purchase_month,
        COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS total_users,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN month_offset = 0 THEN user_id END) AS month_0,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN month_offset = 1 THEN user_id END) AS month_1,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN month_offset = 2 THEN user_id END) AS month_2,
        COUNT(DISTINCT CASE WHEN month_offset = 3 THEN user_id END) AS month_3
    FROM user_months
    GROUP BY first_purchase_month
)
SELECT 
    first_purchase_month,
    ROUND(100.0 * month_0 / total_users, 2) AS pct_month_0,
    ROUND(100.0 * month_1 / total_users, 2) AS pct_month_1,
    ROUND(100.0 * month_2 / total_users, 2) AS pct_month_2,
    ROUND(100.0 * month_3 / total_users, 2) AS pct_month_3
FROM monthly_cohort
ORDER BY first_purchase_month

records.question: Based solely on completed orders, calculate the average monthly percentage growth rate in the number of unique orders (counting distinct order IDs) for each product category by comparing each month's count to the previous month within the same category. Identify the product category with the highest average of these monthly order growth rates. Then, for that specific product category, compute the average monthly revenue growth rate by calculating the percentage change in total revenue (sum of sale prices) from month to month and averaging these values over the entire period.
records.pred_sql:
WITH monthly_orders AS (
    SELECT 
        p.category,
        DATE_TRUNC('MONTH', TO_TIMESTAMP(o.created_at)) AS month,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS unique_orders,
        SUM(oi.sale_price) AS total_revenue
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON oi.product_id = p.id
    WHERE o.status = 'Complete'
    GROUP BY p.category, month
),
monthly_growth AS (
    SELECT 
        category,
        month,
        unique_orders,
        total_revenue,
        LAG(unique_orders) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month) AS prev_unique_orders,
        LAG(total_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month) AS prev_total_revenue
    FROM monthly_orders
),
order_growth_rates AS (
    SELECT 
        category,
        month,
        (unique_orders - prev_unique_orders) / NULLIF(prev_unique_orders, 0) * 100 AS order_growth_rate,
        (total_revenue - prev_total_revenue) / NULLIF(prev_total_revenue, 0) * 100 AS revenue_growth_rate
    FROM monthly_growth
    WHERE prev_unique_orders IS NOT NULL
),
avg_order_growth AS (
    SELECT 
        category,
        AVG(order_growth_rate) AS avg_order_growth_rate
    FROM order_growth_rates
    GROUP BY category
),
top_category AS (
    SELECT category
    FROM avg_order_growth
    ORDER BY avg_order_growth_rate DESC
    LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
    AVG(revenue_growth_rate) AS avg_monthly_revenue_growth_rate
FROM order_growth_rates
WHERE category = (SELECT category FROM top_category)

records.pred_sql:
WITH monthly_profit AS (
    SELECT 
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(oi."created_at")) AS month,
        oi."product_id",
        SUM(oi."sale_price" - ii."cost") AS total_profit,
        SUM(ii."cost") AS total_cost
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.INVENTORY_ITEMS ii ON oi."inventory_item_id" = ii."id"
    WHERE TO_TIMESTAMP(oi."created_at") < '2024-01-01'
    GROUP BY month, oi."product_id"
),
ranked_profit AS (
    SELECT 
        month,
        product_id,
        total_profit,
        total_cost,
        ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY month ORDER BY total_profit DESC) AS rn
    FROM monthly_profit
)
SELECT 
    month,
    product_id,
    total_cost,
    total_profit
FROM ranked_profit
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY month

records.question: Generate a monthly analysis report for e-commerce sales from June 2019 to December 2019 that includes, for each product category and each month, the total number of orders, total revenue, and total profit, along with their month-over-month growth rates using the data from June 2019 as the basis for calculating growth starting from July 2019. Ensure that all orders are included regardless of their status, and present the results sorted in ascending order by month (formatted as "2019-07") and then by product category. Omitting June 2019 from the final output but using it for the growth calculations.
records.pred_sql:
WITH monthly_data AS (
    SELECT 
        p.category,
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(o.created_at))::DATE AS month,
        COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders,
        SUM(oi.sale_price) AS total_revenue,
        SUM(oi.sale_price - p.cost) AS total_profit
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.PRODUCTS p ON oi.product_id = p.id
    WHERE TO_TIMESTAMP(o.created_at) >= '2019-06-01'::TIMESTAMP
      AND TO_TIMESTAMP(o.created_at) < '2020-01-01'::TIMESTAMP
    GROUP BY p.category, DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP(o.created_at))
),
base_data AS (
    SELECT 
        category,
        month,
        total_orders,
        total_revenue,
        total_profit,
        FIRST_VALUE(total_orders) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month) AS base_orders,
        FIRST_VALUE(total_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month) AS base_revenue,
        FIRST_VALUE(total_profit) OVER (PARTITION BY category ORDER BY month) AS base_profit
    FROM monthly_data
)
SELECT 
    TO_CHAR(month, 'YYYY-MM') AS month,
    category,
    total_orders,
    total_revenue,
    total_profit,
    CASE 
        WHEN base_orders = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND((total_orders - base_orders) / base_orders * 100, 2)
    END AS orders_growth_rate,
    CASE 
        WHEN base_revenue = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND((total_revenue - base_revenue) / base_revenue * 100, 2)
    END AS revenue_growth_rate,
    CASE 
        WHEN base_profit = 0 THEN NULL
        ELSE ROUND((total_profit - base_profit) / base_profit * 100, 2)
    END AS profit_growth_rate
FROM base_data
WHERE month >= '2019-07-01'::TIMESTAMP
ORDER BY month, category

    WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM TO_TIMESTAMP(oi."created_at")) = 2020

records.question: Can you list the top 5 months from August 2022 to November 2023 where the profit from Facebook-sourced completed orders showed the largest month-over-month increase? Calculate profit as sales minus costs, group by delivery month, and include only orders created between August 2022 and November 2023. Compare each month's profit to its previous month to find the largest increases.
records.pred_sql:
WITH monthly_profit AS (
    SELECT
        DATE_TRUNC('month', TO_TIMESTAMP_NTZ(o.delivered_at / 1000000)) AS delivery_month,
        SUM(oi.sale_price - ii.cost) AS profit
    FROM THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDERS o
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.ORDER_ITEMS oi ON o.order_id = oi.order_id
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.INVENTORY_ITEMS ii ON oi.inventory_item_id = ii.id
    JOIN THELOOK_ECOMMERCE.USERS u ON o.user_id = u.id
    WHERE o.status = 'Complete'
      AND u.traffic_source = 'Facebook'
      AND o.created_at >= 1659312000000000  -- August 2022 in microseconds
      AND o.created_at < 1701388800000000   -- December 2023 in microseconds
      AND o.delivered_at IS NOT NULL
    GROUP BY delivery_month
),
profit_with_lag AS (
    SELECT
        delivery_month,
        profit,
        LAG(profit) OVER (ORDER BY delivery_month) AS prev_month_profit
    FROM monthly_profit
)
SELECT
    delivery_month,
    profit,
    (profit - prev_month_profit) AS month_over_month_increase
FROM profit_with_lag
WHERE prev_month_profit IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY month_over_month_increase DESC
LIMIT 5

records.question: What is the variant density of the cannabis reference with the longest reference length? Pay attention that a variant is present if there is at least one variant call with a genotype greater than 0.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    COUNT(DISTINCT v.variant_id) * 1.0 / r.length AS variant_density
FROM GENOMICS_CANNABIS.MNPR01_REFERENCE_201703 r
JOIN GENOMICS_CANNABIS.MNPR01_TRANSCRIPTOME_201703 v
    ON v.reference_name = r.name
WHERE EXISTS (
    SELECT 1
    FROM LATERAL FLATTEN(input => v.call) c
    WHERE c.value:GT::NUMBER > 0
)
ORDER BY r.length DESC
LIMIT 1

records.question: Provide a list of the top 10 countries for the year 2020, ordered by the highest percentage of their population under 20 years old. For each country, include the total population under 20 years old, the total midyear population, and the percentage of the population that is under 20 years old.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
  country_name,
  SUM(CASE WHEN age < 20 THEN population ELSE 0 END) AS population_under_20,
  SUM(population) AS total_population,
  ROUND(SUM(CASE WHEN age < 20 THEN population ELSE 0 END) * 100.0 / SUM(population), 2) AS percentage_under_20
FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_international.midyear_population_agespecific`
WHERE year = 2020
GROUP BY country_name
ORDER BY percentage_under_20 DESC
LIMIT 10

records.question: Which country has the highest percentage of population under the age of 25 in 2017?
records.pred_sql:

FROM

WHERE

  AND total_flag IS NULL

records.question: As of May 10, 2020, among all countries that had more than 50,000 confirmed COVID-19 cases, which three countries had the highest recovery rates based on the total number of recovered cases relative to their total confirmed cases, and what were their respective recovery rates expressed as percentages?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  country_name,
  ROUND(100.0 * cumulative_recovered / cumulative_confirmed, 2) AS recovery_rate_percentage
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.covid19_open_data.covid19_open_data`
WHERE
  date = '2020-05-10'
  AND cumulative_confirmed > 50000
  AND cumulative_recovered IS NOT NULL
  AND cumulative_confirmed IS NOT NULL

records.question: You need to calculate the percentage of each country's population that had been confirmed with COVID-19 by June 30, 2020. The population data for 2018 can be found in the World Bank dataset, and the cumulative COVID-19 confirmed cases data is available in the COVID-19 Open Data dataset. Calculate the percentage of each country's population, that was cumulatively confirmed to have COVID-19
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  c.country_name,
  ROUND(100.0 * c.cumulative_confirmed / p.year_2018, 2) AS percent_infected
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.covid19_open_data.covid19_open_data` c
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.world_bank_global_population.population_by_country` p
ON
  c.country_code = p.country_code
WHERE
  c.date = '2020-06-30'
  AND c.aggregation_level = 0
  AND p.year_2018 IS NOT NULL
  AND c.cumulative_confirmed IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY
  percent_infected DESC

records.question: Could you provide, for the United States, France, China, Italy, Spain, Germany, and Iran, the total number of confirmed COVID-19 cases as of April 20, 2020, along with the number of cases per 100,000 people based on their total 2020 populations calculated by summing all relevant population entries from the World Bank data
records.pred_sql:
WITH confirmed_april20 AS (
  SELECT
    country_or_region,
    SUM(CAST(_4_20_20 AS INT64)) AS total_confirmed
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_jhu_csse.confirmed_cases`
  WHERE country_or_region IN ('US', 'France', 'China', 'Italy', 'Spain', 'Germany', 'Iran')
  GROUP BY country_or_region
),
population_2020 AS (
  SELECT
    country_code,
    SUM(year_2020) AS total_population
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.world_bank_global_population.population_by_country`
  WHERE country_code IN (
    SELECT country_code
    FROM `bigquery-public-data.world_bank_health_population.country_summary`
    WHERE short_name IN ('United States', 'France', 'China', 'Italy', 'Spain', 'Germany', 'Iran')
  )
  GROUP BY country_code
)
SELECT
  c.country_or_region,
  c.total_confirmed,
  ROUND(c.total_confirmed * 100000.0 / p.total_population, 2) AS cases_per_100k
FROM confirmed_april20 c
JOIN population_2020 p
  ON c.country_or_region = (
    SELECT short_name
    FROM `bigquery-public-data.world_bank_health_population.country_summary`
    WHERE country_code = p.country_code
  )
ORDER BY c.country_or_region

records.question: Analyze daily new COVID-19 case counts from March to May 2020, identifying the top five states by daily increases. Please compile a ranking based on how often each state appears in these daily top fives. Then, examine the state that ranks fourth overall and identify its top five counties based on their frequency of appearing in the daily top five new case counts.
records.pred_sql:
WITH daily_state_cases AS (
  SELECT
    state_name,
    date,
    confirmed_cases - LAG(confirmed_cases) OVER (PARTITION BY state_name ORDER BY date) AS new_cases
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_nyt.us_states`
  WHERE date BETWEEN '2020-03-01' AND '2020-05-31'
),
daily_top5 AS (
  SELECT
    date,
    state_name,
    new_cases,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY date ORDER BY new_cases DESC) AS rn
  FROM daily_state_cases
  WHERE new_cases IS NOT NULL
),
state_rankings AS (
  SELECT
    state_name,
    COUNT(*) AS top5_frequency
  FROM daily_top5
  WHERE rn <= 5
  GROUP BY state_name
),
fourth_state AS (
  SELECT state_name
  FROM state_rankings
  ORDER BY top5_frequency DESC
  LIMIT 1 OFFSET 3
),
daily_county_cases AS (
  SELECT
    county,
    state_name,
    date,
    confirmed_cases - LAG(confirmed_cases) OVER (PARTITION BY county, state_name ORDER BY date) AS new_cases
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_nyt.us_counties`
  WHERE date BETWEEN '2020-03-01' AND '2020-05-31'
    AND state_name = (SELECT state_name FROM fourth_state)
),
daily_county_top5 AS (
  SELECT
    date,
    county,
    new_cases,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY date ORDER BY new_cases DESC) AS rn
  FROM daily_county_cases
  WHERE new_cases IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  county,
  COUNT(*) AS top5_frequency
FROM daily_county_top5
WHERE rn <= 5
GROUP BY county
ORDER BY top5_frequency DESC
LIMIT 5

records.question: Please calculate the overall percentage change in the average weekly search frequency for the symptom 'Anosmia' across the five New York City counties—Bronx County, Queens County, Kings County, New York County, and Richmond County—by comparing the combined data from January 1, 2019, through December 31, 2019, with the combined data from January 1, 2020, through December 31, 2020.
records.pred_sql:
WITH avg_2019 AS (
  SELECT AVG(SAFE_CAST(symptom_anosmia AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_2019
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_symptom_search.symptom_search_sub_region_1_weekly`
  WHERE country_region = 'United States'
    AND sub_region_1 IN ('Bronx County', 'Queens County', 'Kings County', 'New York County', 'Richmond County')
    AND date >= '2019-01-01'
    AND date <= '2019-12-31'
    AND SAFE_CAST(symptom_anosmia AS FLOAT64) IS NOT NULL
),
avg_2020 AS (
  SELECT AVG(SAFE_CAST(symptom_anosmia AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_2020
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_symptom_search.symptom_search_sub_region_1_weekly`
  WHERE country_region = 'United States'
    AND sub_region_1 IN ('Bronx County', 'Queens County', 'Kings County', 'New York County', 'Richmond County')
    AND date >= '2020-01-01'
    AND date <= '2020-12-31'
    AND SAFE_CAST(symptom_anosmia AS FLOAT64) IS NOT NULL
)
SELECT
  ROUND(((avg_2020.avg_2020 - avg_2019.avg_2019) / avg_2019.avg_2019) * 100, 2) AS percentage_change
FROM avg_2019, avg_2020

records.question: Please calculate the average levels of anxiety and depression symptoms from the weekly country data for the United States during the periods from January 1, 2019, to January 1, 2020, and from January 1, 2020, to January 1, 2021. Then, compute the percentage increase in these average symptom levels from the 2019 period to the 2020 period.
records.pred_sql:
WITH pre_covid AS (
  SELECT 
    AVG(CAST(symptom_anxiety AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_anxiety_pre,
    AVG(CAST(symptom_depression AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_depression_pre
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_symptom_search.symptom_search_country_weekly`
  WHERE country_region = 'United States'
    AND date >= '2019-01-01'
    AND date < '2020-01-01'
),
post_covid AS (
  SELECT 
    AVG(CAST(symptom_anxiety AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_anxiety_post,
    AVG(CAST(symptom_depression AS FLOAT64)) AS avg_depression_post
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_symptom_search.symptom_search_country_weekly`
  WHERE country_region = 'United States'
    AND date >= '2020-01-01'
    AND date < '2021-01-01'
)
SELECT 
  pre.avg_anxiety_pre,
  pre.avg_depression_pre,
  post.avg_anxiety_post,
  post.avg_depression_post,
  ((post.avg_anxiety_post - pre.avg_anxiety_pre) / pre.avg_anxiety_pre) * 100 AS anxiety_pct_increase,
  ((post.avg_depression_post - pre.avg_depression_pre) / pre.avg_depression_pre) * 100 AS depression_pct_increase
FROM pre_covid pre
CROSS JOIN post_covid post

records.question: Given the latest population estimates from the 2018 five-year American Community Survey, what is the number of vaccine sites per 1000 people for counties in California?
records.pred_sql:
WITH vaccine_sites AS (
  SELECT 
    state,
    county,
    COUNT(*) AS site_count
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_usa.covid19_vaccine_sites`
  WHERE state = 'California'
  GROUP BY state, county
),
population AS (
  SELECT 
    geo_id,
    total_pop
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.censustract_2018_5yr`
)
SELECT 
  v.county,
  ROUND((v.site_count / p.total_pop) * 1000, 2) AS sites_per_1000_people
FROM vaccine_sites v
JOIN population p
  ON v.county = p.geo_id
ORDER BY sites_per_1000_people DESC

records.question: Find the top three counties with populations over 50,000, using the 2020 5-year census data, that had the highest COVID-19 case fatality rates on August 27, 2020. For these counties, provide the name, state, median age, total population, number of confirmed COVID-19 cases per 100,000 people, number of deaths per 100,000 people, and the case fatality rate as a percentage
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  county.name,
  county.state_name,
  acs.median_age,
  acs.total_population,
  (covid.confirmed_cases / acs.total_population) * 100000 AS cases_per_100k,
  (covid.deaths / acs.total_population) * 100000 AS deaths_per_100k,
  (covid.deaths / covid.confirmed_cases) * 100 AS case_fatality_rate_pct
FROM (
  SELECT
    geo_id,
    median_age,
    total_population
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.county_2020_5yr`
  WHERE total_population > 50000
) AS acs
JOIN (
  SELECT
    county_fips_code,
    confirmed_cases,
    deaths
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.covid19_usa.county_daily`
  WHERE date = '2020-08-27'
    AND confirmed_cases > 0
) AS covid
  ON acs.geo_id = covid.county_fips_code
JOIN (
  SELECT
    fips_code,
    name,
    state_name
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.county_2020_5yr`
) AS county
  ON acs.geo_id = county.fips_code
ORDER BY case_fatality_rate_pct DESC
LIMIT 3

records.question: Please find all zip code areas located within 10 kilometers of the coordinates (-122.3321, 47.6062) by joining the 2010 census population data (summing only male and female populations with no age constraints) and the zip code area information, and return each area’s polygon, land and water area in meters, latitude and longitude, state code, state name, city, county, and total population.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  z.zipcode_geom,
  z.area_land_meters,
  z.area_water_meters,
  z.latitude,
  z.longitude,
  z.state_code,
  z.state_name,
  z.city,
  z.county,
  SUM(p.population) AS total_population
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.utility_us.zipcode_area` z
JOIN
  `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_usa.population_by_zip_2010` p
ON
  z.zipcode = p.zipcode
WHERE
  ST_DWITHIN(
    ST_GEOGPOINT(z.longitude, z.latitude),
    ST_GEOGPOINT(-122.3321, 47.6062),
    10000
  )
  AND p.gender IN ('male', 'female')
  AND p.minimum_age IS NULL
  AND p.maximum_age IS NULL
GROUP BY
  z.zipcode,
  z.zipcode_geom,
  z.area_land_meters,
  z.area_water_meters,
  z.latitude,
  z.longitude,
  z.state_code,
  z.state_name,
  z.city,
  z.county

records.question: Can you find the census tracts in the 36047 area that are among the top 20 for the largest percentage increases in population from 2011 to 2018, are also among the top 20 for the largest absolute increases in median income during the same period, and had over 1,000 residents in each of those years?
records.pred_sql:
WITH pop_2011 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, total_pop AS pop_2011
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.censustract_2011_5yr`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '36047%'
),
pop_2018 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, total_pop AS pop_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.censustract_2018_5yr`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '36047%'
),
income_2011 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, median_income AS income_2011
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.censustract_2011_5yr`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '36047%'
),
income_2018 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, median_income AS income_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs.censustract_2018_5yr`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '36047%'
),
combined AS (
  SELECT p11.geo_id,
         p11.pop_2011,
         p18.pop_2018,
         i11.income_2011,
         i18.income_2018,
         (p18.pop_2018 - p11.pop_2011) / p11.pop_2011 * 100 AS pct_pop_change,
         i18.income_2018 - i11.income_2011 AS abs_income_change
  FROM pop_2011 p11
  JOIN pop_2018 p18 ON p11.geo_id = p18.geo_id
  JOIN income_2011 i11 ON p11.geo_id = i11.geo_id
  JOIN income_2018 i18 ON p11.geo_id = i18.geo_id
  WHERE p11.pop_2011 > 1000 AND p18.pop_2018 > 1000
),
top_pop AS (
  SELECT geo_id
  FROM combined
  ORDER BY pct_pop_change DESC
  LIMIT 20
),
top_income AS (
  SELECT geo_id
  FROM combined
  ORDER BY abs_income_change DESC
  LIMIT 20
)
SELECT c.geo_id,
       c.pop_2011,
       c.pop_2018,
       c.income_2011,
       c.income_2018,
       ROUND(c.pct_pop_change, 2) AS pct_pop_change,
       c.abs_income_change
FROM combined c
JOIN top_pop tp ON c.geo_id = tp.geo_id
JOIN top_income ti ON c.geo_id = ti.geo_id
ORDER BY c.pct_pop_change DESC

records.question: Which census tract has witnessed the largest increase in median income between 2015 and 2018 in California? Tell me the tract code.
records.pred_sql:
WITH income_2015 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, median_income AS income_2015
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs_1.census_1_2015`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '06%'
),
income_2018 AS (
  SELECT geo_id, median_income AS income_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs_1.census_1_2018`
  WHERE geo_id LIKE '06%'
)
SELECT t.tract_ce AS tract_code
FROM income_2015 i15
JOIN income_2018 i18 ON i15.geo_id = i18.geo_id
JOIN `bigquery-public-data.geo_census_tracts.census_tracts_california` t ON i15.geo_id = t.geo_id
ORDER BY (i18.income_2018 - i15.income_2015) DESC
LIMIT 1

records.question: Using the 2017 U.S. Census Tract data from the BigQuery public datasets, you need to proportionally allocate each tract's population and income to the zip codes based on the overlapping area between their geographic boundaries. Then, filter the results to include only those zip codes located within a 5-mile radius of a specific point in Washington State, with coordinates at latitude 47.685833°N and longitude -122.191667°W. Finally, calculate the total population and the average individual income for each zip code (rounded to one decimal place) and sort the results by the average individual income in descending order.
records.pred_sql:
WITH washington_tracts AS (
  SELECT
    geo_id,
    tract_geom,
    area_land_meters
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.geo_census_tracts.census_tracts_washington`
),
zip_codes_near_point AS (
  SELECT
    zip_code,
    zip_code_geom
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.zip_codes`
  WHERE ST_DWITHIN(
    zip_code_geom,
    ST_GEOGPOINT(-122.191667, 47.685833),
    5 * 1609.34
  )
),
intersections AS (
  SELECT
    z.zip_code,
    t.geo_id,
    ST_AREA(ST_INTERSECT(t.tract_geom, z.zip_code_geom)) AS overlap_area,
    t.area_land_meters AS tract_area
  FROM washington_tracts t
  JOIN zip_codes_near_point z
    ON ST_INTERSECTS(t.tract_geom, z.zip_code_geom)
),
overlap_fractions AS (
  SELECT
    zip_code,
    geo_id,
    overlap_area / tract_area AS fraction
  FROM intersections
),
census_data AS (
  SELECT
    geo_id,
    total_pop,
    income_per_capita
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.census_bureau_acs_1.census_1_2017`
)
SELECT
  o.zip_code,
  ROUND(SUM(o.fraction * c.total_pop), 1) AS total_population,
  ROUND(AVG(c.income_per_capita), 1) AS avg_individual_income
FROM overlap_fractions o
JOIN census_data c
  ON o.geo_id = c.geo_id
GROUP BY o.zip_code
ORDER BY avg_individual_income DESC

records.question: Please generate a table from the NCAA basketball dataset that lists the top five records in each of these four categories: (1) Top Venues - the largest venues by seating capacity with Date shown as 'N/A'; (2) Biggest Championship Margins - National Championship games since the 2016 season (season > 2015) with the biggest point margin victories; (3) Highest Scoring Games - games since the 2011 season (season > 2010) with the highest total points scored by both teams combined; and (4) Total Threes - games since the 2011 season (season > 2010) with the highest total three-pointers made by both teams combined. The final table should be organized with columns for Category, Date, Matchup or Venue, and Key Metric, with each category's 5 records presented in descending order of their key metric.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 'Top Venues' AS Category, 'N/A' AS Date, venue_name AS Matchup_or_Venue, CAST(venue_capacity AS STRING) AS Key_Metric
FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams`
WHERE venue_capacity IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY venue_capacity DESC
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Biggest Championship Margins' AS Category, FORMAT_DATE('%Y-%m-%d', scheduled_date) AS Date, CONCAT(h_market, ' vs ', a_market) AS Matchup_or_Venue, CAST(ABS(h_score - a_score) AS STRING) AS Key_Metric
FROM (
  SELECT g.scheduled_date, g.h_market, g.h_alias, g.a_market, g.a_alias,
         (SELECT SUM(points) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS points)])) AS h_score,
         (SELECT SUM(points) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS points)])) AS a_score
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_games_sr` g
  WHERE g.season > 2015 AND g.tournament = 'NCAA' AND g.tournament_round = 'National Championship'
) sub
ORDER BY CAST(Key_Metric AS INT64) DESC
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Highest Scoring Games' AS Category, FORMAT_DATE('%Y-%m-%d', scheduled_date) AS Date, CONCAT(h_market, ' vs ', a_market) AS Matchup_or_Venue, CAST((h_score + a_score) AS STRING) AS Key_Metric
FROM (
  SELECT g.scheduled_date, g.h_market, g.a_market,
         (SELECT SUM(points) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS points)])) AS h_score,
         (SELECT SUM(points) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS points)])) AS a_score
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_games_sr` g
  WHERE g.season > 2010
) sub
ORDER BY CAST(Key_Metric AS INT64) DESC
LIMIT 5

UNION ALL

SELECT 'Total Threes' AS Category, FORMAT_DATE('%Y-%m-%d', scheduled_date) AS Date, CONCAT(h_market, ' vs ', a_market) AS Matchup_or_Venue, CAST((h_three + a_three) AS STRING) AS Key_Metric
FROM (
  SELECT g.scheduled_date, g.h_market, g.a_market,
         (SELECT SUM(three_points_made) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS three_points_made)])) AS h_three,
         (SELECT SUM(three_points_made) FROM UNNEST([STRUCT(CAST(0 AS INT64) AS three_points_made)])) AS a_three
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_games_sr` g
  WHERE g.season > 2010
) sub
ORDER BY CAST(Key_Metric AS INT64) DESC
LIMIT 5

records.question: Could you determine, for each shot type, the average x and y coordinates (adjusted to ensure consistency regarding the left or right basket), the average number of shot attempts, and the average number of successful shots, considering only shots taken before March 15, 2018, excluding those with null shot types or coordinates, ensuring the shots are on the correct side of the court based on the team's basket.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
  shot_type,
  AVG(CASE
    WHEN event_coord_x < 564 THEN event_coord_x
    ELSE 1128 - event_coord_x
  END) AS avg_x,
  AVG(CASE
    WHEN event_coord_x < 564 THEN 600 - event_coord_y
    ELSE event_coord_y
  END) AS avg_y,
  AVG(shot_attempts) AS avg_shot_attempts,
  AVG(shot_made) AS avg_shots_made
FROM
  `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_pbp_sr`
WHERE
  scheduled_date < '2018-03-15'
  AND shot_type IS NOT NULL
  AND event_coord_x IS NOT NULL
  AND event_coord_y IS NOT NULL
  AND (
    (team_basket = 'left' AND event_coord_x < 564)
    OR (team_basket = 'right' AND event_coord_x >= 564)
  )
GROUP BY
  shot_type
ORDER BY
  shot_type

records.question: For the top five team markets with the highest number of distinct players who scored at least 15 points during the second period of games between 2010 and 2018, provide details of each game they played in NCAA basketball historical tournament matches during the same period, as specified in the data model document.
records.pred_sql:
WITH top_teams AS (
  SELECT 
    tg.market,
    COUNT(DISTINCT pg.player_id) AS distinct_players
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_players_games_sr` pg
  JOIN `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_teams_games_sr` tg
    ON pg.game_id = tg.game_id AND pg.team_id = tg.team_id
  WHERE pg.season BETWEEN 2010 AND 2018
    AND pg.played = TRUE
    AND pg.pts >= 15
    AND pg.period = 2
  GROUP BY tg.market
  ORDER BY distinct_players DESC
  LIMIT 5
)
SELECT 
  htg.season,
  htg.round,
  htg.game_date,
  htg.day,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN 'win'
    ELSE 'loss'
  END AS label,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.win_seed
    ELSE htg.lose_seed
  END AS seed,
  t.market,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.win_name
    ELSE htg.lose_name
  END AS name,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.win_alias
    ELSE htg.lose_alias
  END AS alias,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.win_school_ncaa
    ELSE htg.lose_school_ncaa
  END AS school_ncaa,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.lose_seed
    ELSE htg.win_seed
  END AS opponent_seed,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.lose_market
    ELSE htg.win_market
  END AS opponent_market,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.lose_name
    ELSE htg.win_name
  END AS opponent_name,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.lose_alias
    ELSE htg.win_alias
  END AS opponent_alias,
  CASE 
    WHEN htg.win_market = t.market THEN htg.lose_school_ncaa
    ELSE htg.win_school_ncaa
  END AS opponent_school_ncaa
FROM top_teams t
JOIN `bigquery-public-data.ncaa_basketball.mbb_historical_tournament_games` htg
  ON (htg.win_market = t.market OR htg.lose_market = t.market)
WHERE htg.season BETWEEN 2010 AND 2018
ORDER BY t.market, htg.game_date

records.question: Create a dataset by combining NCAA men's basketball tournament game outcomes from the 2014 season onwards, including both the historical tournament games and the 2018 tournament results, with the corresponding pace and efficiency performance metrics for each team and their opponents from the feature_engineering data. The dataset should include the season, game outcome labels (win or loss), team and opponent seeds, school names, pace and efficiency rankings, statistical values, and the differences between the team's and the opponent's metrics to enable a comprehensive analysis of team and opponent dynamics.
records.pred_sql:
WITH historical_games AS (

    'win' AS label,

    lose_school_ncaa AS opponent_school_ncaa
  FROM `data-to-insights.ncaa.mbb_historical_tournament_games`
  WHERE season >= 2014
  UNION ALL
  SELECT
    season,
    'loss' AS label,
    lose_seed AS seed,
    lose_school_ncaa AS school_ncaa,
    win_seed AS opponent_seed,
    win_school_ncaa AS opponent_school_ncaa
  FROM `data-to-insights.ncaa.mbb_historical_tournament_games`
  WHERE season >= 2014
),
tournament_2018 AS (
  SELECT
    season,
    label,
    seed,
    school_ncaa,
    opponent_seed,
    opponent_school_ncaa
  FROM `data-to-insights.ncaa.2018_tournament_results`

combined_games AS (

SELECT
  c.season,

  opp.efficiency_rating AS opp_efficiency_rating,
  opp.pace_rank - team.pace_rank AS pace_rank_diff,
  opp.poss_40min - team.poss_40min AS pace_stat_diff,
  opp.pace_rating - team.pace_rating AS pace_rating_diff,
  opp.efficiency_rank - team.efficiency_rank AS eff_rank_diff,

records.question: Which county in Utah experienced the greatest percentage increase in construction employment from 2000 to 2018, calculated by averaging the employment levels during the third month of each quarter in those years? What is the corresponding percentage increase?
records.pred_sql:
WITH construction_2000 AS (
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2000
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2000_q1`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2000
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2000_q2`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2000
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2000_q3`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2000
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2000_q4`
  GROUP BY area_fips
),
construction_2018 AS (
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2018_q1`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2018_q2`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2018_q3`
  GROUP BY area_fips
  UNION ALL
  SELECT 
    area_fips,
    AVG(month3_emplvl_23_construction) AS avg_empl_2018
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.2018_q4`
  GROUP BY area_fips
),
avg_2000 AS (
  SELECT area_fips, AVG(avg_empl_2000) AS avg_2000
  FROM construction_2000
  GROUP BY area_fips
),
avg_2018 AS (
  SELECT area_fips, AVG(avg_empl_2018) AS avg_2018
  FROM construction_2018
  GROUP BY area_fips
)
SELECT 
  a.area_fips,
  (a.avg_2018 - b.avg_2000) / b.avg_2000 * 100 AS pct_increase
FROM avg_2018 a
JOIN avg_2000 b ON a.area_fips = b.area_fips
WHERE b.avg_2000 > 0
  AND a.area_fips LIKE '49%'
ORDER BY pct_increase DESC
LIMIT 1

records.question: Between 1998 and 2017, for Allegheny County in the Pittsburgh area, did the average annual wages for all industries keep pace with the inflation of all consumer items, and what were the respective percentage growth rates (to two decimal places) for wages and the CPI over that period?
records.pred_sql:
WITH wage_data AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    AVG(avg_wkly_wage_1023_financi) AS avg_weekly_wage
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls_qcew.*`
  WHERE _TABLE_SUFFIX BETWEEN '1998_q1' AND '2017_q4'
    AND area_fips = '42003'
  GROUP BY year
),
annual_wage AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    AVG(avg_weekly_wage) * 52 AS avg_annual_wage
  FROM wage_data
  GROUP BY year
),
cpi_data AS (
  SELECT 
    year,
    AVG(value) AS avg_cpi
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.bls.c_cpi_u`
  WHERE area_code = '0'
    AND item_code = 'SA0'
    AND year BETWEEN 1998 AND 2017
  GROUP BY year
),
base_values AS (
  SELECT 
    w.year,
    w.avg_annual_wage,
    c.avg_cpi
  FROM annual_wage w
  JOIN cpi_data c ON w.year = c.year
),
growth_rates AS (
  SELECT
    (MAX(CASE WHEN year = 2017 THEN avg_annual_wage END) - MIN(CASE WHEN year = 1998 THEN avg_annual_wage END)) / MIN(CASE WHEN year = 1998 THEN avg_annual_wage END) * 100 AS wage_growth_pct,
    (MAX(CASE WHEN year = 2017 THEN avg_cpi END) - MIN(CASE WHEN year = 1998 THEN avg_cpi END)) / MIN(CASE WHEN year = 1998 THEN avg_cpi END) * 100 AS cpi_growth_pct
  FROM base_values
)
SELECT 
  ROUND(wage_growth_pct, 2) AS wage_growth_percentage,
  ROUND(cpi_growth_pct, 2) AS cpi_growth_percentage,
  CASE 
    WHEN wage_growth_pct >= cpi_growth_pct THEN 'Wages kept pace with inflation'
    ELSE 'Wages did not keep pace with inflation'
  END AS conclusion
FROM growth_rates

records.question: Please calculate the growth rates for Asians, Black people, Latinx people, Native Americans, White people, US women, US men, global women, and global men from 2014 to 2024 concerning the overall workforce.
records.pred_sql:
WITH base AS (
  SELECT 
    report_year,
    race_asian,
    race_black,
    race_hispanic_latinx,
    race_native_american,
    race_white,
    gender_us_women,
    gender_us_men,
    gender_global_women,
    gender_global_men
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.google_dei.dar_non_intersectional_representation`
  WHERE workforce = 'overall'
    AND report_year IN (2014, 2024)
)
SELECT 
  'Asian' AS group_name,
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN race_asian END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_asian END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_asian END) AS growth_rate
FROM base
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'Black',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN race_black END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_black END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_black END)

UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'Latinx',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN race_hispanic_latinx END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_hispanic_latinx END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_hispanic_latinx END)
FROM base
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'Native American',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN race_native_american END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_native_american END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_native_american END)
FROM base
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'White',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN race_white END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_white END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN race_white END)
FROM base
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'US Women',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN gender_us_women END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_us_women END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_us_women END)
FROM base
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'US Men',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN gender_us_men END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_us_men END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_us_men END)
FROM base

SELECT 
  'Global Women',
  (MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2024 THEN gender_global_women END) - MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_global_women END)) / MAX(CASE WHEN report_year = 2014 THEN gender_global_women END)

SELECT

records.question: For each month in the year 2023, how many total transactions occurred (counting all transaction records without removing duplicates of transaction hashes), and how many transactions per second were processed each month, where the transactions-per-second value is calculated by dividing the monthly total count by the exact number of seconds in that month, including the correct leap-year logic if applicable based on the extracted year from the transaction timestamp? records.pred_sql:

    COUNT(*) AS monthly_transaction_count,
    COUNT(*) / (CASE 
        WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM "block_timestamp") IN (1,3,5,7,8,10,12) THEN 31 * 86400
        WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM "block_timestamp") IN (4,6,9,11) THEN 30 * 86400
        WHEN EXTRACT(MONTH FROM "block_timestamp") = 2 THEN 
            CASE 
                WHEN (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "block_timestamp") % 4 = 0 AND EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "block_timestamp") % 100 != 0) 
                     OR (EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "block_timestamp") % 400 = 0) THEN 29 * 86400

FROM GOOG_BLOCKCHAIN_CRONOS_MAINNET_US.TRANSACTIONS
WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM "block_timestamp") = 2023

UNION ALL

records.question: Calculate the minimum and maximum trip duration in minutes (rounded to the nearest whole number), total number of trips, and average fare for each of six equal quantile groups based on trip duration, considering only trips between 0 and 60 minutes.
records.pred_sql:
WITH trip_data AS (
  SELECT
    trip_seconds,
    fare,
    trip_seconds / 60 AS trip_duration_minutes
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.chicago_taxi_trips.taxi_trips`
  WHERE trip_seconds IS NOT NULL
    AND trip_seconds > 0
    AND trip_seconds <= 3600
    AND fare IS NOT NULL
),
quantiles AS (
  SELECT
    *,
    NTILE(6) OVER (ORDER BY trip_duration_minutes) AS quantile_group
  FROM trip_data
)
SELECT
  quantile_group,
  ROUND(MIN(trip_duration_minutes)) AS min_duration_minutes,
  ROUND(MAX(trip_duration_minutes)) AS max_duration_minutes,
  COUNT(*) AS total_trips,
  ROUND(AVG(fare), 2) AS average_fare
FROM quantiles
GROUP BY quantile_group
ORDER BY quantile_group

records.question: Which three companies had the largest increase in trip numbers between two consecutive months in 2018?
records.pred_sql:
WITH monthly_trips AS (
  SELECT
    company,
    FORMAT_TIMESTAMP('%Y-%m', trip_start_timestamp) AS month,
    COUNT(*) AS trip_count
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.chicago_taxi_trips.taxi_trips`
  WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM trip_start_timestamp) = 2018
  GROUP BY company, month
),
monthly_diff AS (
  SELECT
    company,
    month,
    trip_count,
    LAG(trip_count) OVER (PARTITION BY company ORDER BY month) AS prev_trip_count
  FROM monthly_trips
)
SELECT
  company,
  month,
  trip_count - prev_trip_count AS increase
FROM monthly_diff
WHERE prev_trip_count IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY increase DESC
LIMIT 3

records.question: Calculate the total number of trips and average fare (formatted to two decimal places) for ten equal-sized quantile groups. Each quantile group should contain approximately the same number of trips based on their rounded trip duration in minutes (between 1-50 minutes). Display each group's time range formatted as "XXm to XXm" (where the numbers are zero-padded to two digits), the total trips count, and the average fare. The time ranges should represent the minimum and maximum duration values within each quantile. Sort the results chronologically by time range. Use NTILE(10) to create the quantiles from the ordered trip durations.
records.pred_sql:
WITH trip_durations AS (
  SELECT 
    trip_seconds,
    ROUND(trip_seconds / 60) AS duration_minutes,
    fare
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.chicago_taxi_trips.taxi_trips`
  WHERE trip_seconds IS NOT NULL
    AND trip_seconds BETWEEN 60 AND 3000
),
quantiles AS (
  SELECT 
    duration_minutes,
    fare,
    NTILE(10) OVER (ORDER BY duration_minutes) AS quantile_group
  FROM trip_durations
),
group_stats AS (
  SELECT 
    quantile_group,
    MIN(duration_minutes) AS min_duration,
    MAX(duration_minutes) AS max_duration,
    COUNT(*) AS total_trips,
    ROUND(AVG(fare), 2) AS avg_fare
  FROM quantiles
  GROUP BY quantile_group
)
SELECT 
  CONCAT(
    LPAD(CAST(min_duration AS STRING), 2, '0'), 'm to ',
    LPAD(CAST(max_duration AS STRING), 2, '0'), 'm'
  ) AS time_range,
  total_trips,
  avg_fare
FROM group_stats
ORDER BY min_duration

records.question: How much higher the average intrinsic value is for trades using the feeling-lucky strategy compared to those using the momentum strategy under long-side trades?
records.pred_sql:
WITH long_sides AS (
  SELECT
    TradeReportID,
    Symbol,
    StrikePrice,
    LastPx,
    Sides
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.cymbal_investments.trade_capture_report`,
  UNNEST(Sides) AS side
  WHERE side.Side = 'LONG'
),
intrinsic_value AS (
  SELECT
    TradeReportID,
    Symbol,
    StrikePrice,
    LastPx,
    (StrikePrice - LastPx) AS intrinsic_value,
    Sides
  FROM long_sides
),
strategy_classification AS (
  SELECT
    TradeReportID,
    Symbol,
    intrinsic_value,
    CASE
      WHEN Symbol IN ('AAPL', 'GOOGL', 'MSFT') THEN 'feeling-lucky'
      WHEN Symbol IN ('TSLA', 'AMZN', 'NVDA') THEN 'momentum'
      ELSE 'other'
    END AS strategy
  FROM intrinsic_value
)
SELECT
  ROUND(AVG(CASE WHEN strategy = 'feeling-lucky' THEN intrinsic_value END) - AVG(CASE WHEN strategy = 'momentum' THEN intrinsic_value END), 2) AS avg_intrinsic_value_difference
FROM strategy_classification
WHERE strategy IN ('feeling-lucky', 'momentum')

    WHEN SUBSTR(TargetCompID, 1, 4) = 'MOMO' THEN 'Momentum'
    WHEN SUBSTR(TargetCompID, 1, 4) = 'LUCK' THEN 'Feeling Lucky'
    WHEN SUBSTR(TargetCompID, 1, 4) = 'PRED' THEN 'Prediction'
  END AS algorithm,
  Symbol AS symbol,

records.question: Considering only the latest evaluation group per state for the 'EXPCURR' evaluation type, determine which state has the highest total acreage of timberland and which has the highest total acreage of forestland. For timberland, include plots where the condition status code is 1, the reserved status code is 0, and the site productivity class code is between 1 and 6. For forestland, include plots where the condition status code is 1. Calculate the total acres by summing the adjusted expansion factors for macroplots and subplots, using their respective proportion bases ('MACR' for macroplots and 'SUBP' for subplots) and adjustment factors when greater than zero. For each category (timberland and forestland), provide the state code, evaluation group, state name, and the total acres for the state with the highest total acreage, considering only the latest evaluation group per state.
records.pred_sql:
WITH latest_eval_group AS (
  SELECT 
    peg.state_code,
    peg.evaluation_group,
    peg.evaluation_group_description,
    peg.evaluation_group_sequence_number,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY peg.state_code ORDER BY peg.evaluation_group DESC) AS rn
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia.population_evaluation_group` peg
  JOIN `bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia.population_evaluation` pe
    ON peg.evaluation_group_sequence_number = pe.evaluation_group_sequence_number
  WHERE pe.evaluation_identifier IN (
    SELECT evaluation_identifier 
    FROM `bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia.population_evaluation` 
    WHERE evaluation_description LIKE '%EXPCURR%'
  )
),
filtered_population AS (
  SELECT 
    pop.state_code,
    pop.evaluation_group_sequence_number,
    pop.plot_sequence_number,
    pop.estimation_unit,
    pop.stratum_code,
    pop.area_used_to_calculate_all_expansion_factors AS area_total,
    pop.adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot AS adj_macr,
    pop.adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot AS adj_subp,
    pop.adjustment_factor_for_the_microplot AS adj_micr
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia.population` pop
  INNER JOIN latest_eval_group leg
    ON pop.state_code = leg.state_code 
    AND pop.evaluation_group_sequence_number = leg.evaluation_group_sequence_number
    AND leg.rn = 1
),
plot_condition AS (
  SELECT 
    c.plot_sequence_number,
    c.state_code,
    c.condition_class_number,
    c.condition_status_code,
    c.reserved_status_code,
    c.site_productivity_class_code,
    c.proportion_basis,
    c.condition_proportion_unadjusted AS cond_prop_unadj
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.usfs_fia.condition` c
  WHERE c.condition_status_code = 1
),
timberland_plots AS (
  SELECT 
    pc.plot_sequence_number,
    pc.state_code,
    pc.condition_class_number,
    pc.cond_prop_unadj,
    pc.proportion_basis,
    fp.area_total,
    fp.adj_macr,
    fp.adj_subp,
    fp.adj_micr
  FROM plot_condition pc
  JOIN filtered_population fp
    ON pc.plot_sequence_number = fp.plot_sequence_number
  WHERE pc.reserved_status_code = 0
    AND pc.site_productivity_class_code BETWEEN 1 AND 6
),
forestland_plots AS (
  SELECT 
    pc.plot_sequence_number,
    pc.state_code,
    pc.condition_class_number,
    pc.cond_prop_unadj,
    pc.proportion_basis,
    fp.area_total,
    fp.adj_macr,
    fp.adj_subp,
    fp.adj_micr
  FROM plot_condition pc
  JOIN filtered_population fp
    ON pc.plot_sequence_number = fp.plot_sequence_number
),
timberland_acres AS (
  SELECT 
    state_code,
    SUM(
      CASE 
        WHEN proportion_basis = 'MACR' AND adj_macr > 0 THEN cond_prop_unadj * area_total * adj_macr
        WHEN proportion_basis = 'SUBP' AND adj_subp > 0 THEN cond_prop_unadj * area_total * adj_subp
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS total_acres
  FROM timberland_plots
  GROUP BY state_code
),
forestland_acres AS (
  SELECT 
    state_code,
    SUM(
      CASE 
        WHEN proportion_basis = 'MACR' AND adj_macr > 0 THEN cond_prop_unadj * area_total * adj_macr
        WHEN proportion_basis = 'SUBP' AND adj_subp > 0 THEN cond_prop_unadj * area_total * adj_subp
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS total_acres
  FROM forestland_plots
  GROUP BY state_code
),
max_timberland AS (
  SELECT 
    ta.state_code,
    leg.evaluation_group,
    leg.evaluation_group_description AS state_name,
    ta.total_acres
  FROM timberland_acres ta
  JOIN latest_eval_group leg ON ta.state_code = leg.state_code AND leg.rn = 1
  ORDER BY ta.total_acres DESC
  LIMIT 1
),
max_forestland AS (
  SELECT 
    fa.state_code,
    leg.evaluation_group,
    leg.evaluation_group_description AS state_name,
    fa.total_acres
  FROM forestland_acres fa
  JOIN latest_eval_group leg ON fa.state_code = leg.state_code AND leg.rn = 1
  ORDER BY fa.total_acres DESC
  LIMIT 1
)
SELECT 
  'Timberland' AS category,
  state_code,
  evaluation_group,
  state_name,
  total_acres
FROM max_timberland
UNION ALL
SELECT 
  'Forestland' AS category,
  state_code,
  evaluation_group,
  state_name,
  total_acres
FROM max_forestland

    AND c.inventory_year IN (2015, 2016, 2017)
),
subplot_sizes AS (
  SELECT
    state_code,
    state_code_name,
    inventory_year,
    expansion_factor * condition_proportion_unadjusted * adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot AS subplot_size
  FROM plot_data
  WHERE proportion_basis = 'SUBP'
    AND adjustment_factor_for_the_subplot > 0
),
macroplot_sizes AS (
  SELECT
    state_code,
    state_code_name,
    inventory_year,
    expansion_factor * condition_proportion_unadjusted * adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot AS macroplot_size
  FROM plot_data
  WHERE proportion_basis = 'MACR'
    AND adjustment_factor_for_the_macroplot > 0
),
avg_subplot AS (
  SELECT
    inventory_year,
    state_code_name AS state,
    AVG(subplot_size) AS avg_size
  FROM subplot_sizes
  GROUP BY inventory_year, state_code_name
),
avg_macroplot AS (
  SELECT
    inventory_year,
    state_code_name AS state,
    AVG(macroplot_size) AS avg_size
  FROM macroplot_sizes
  GROUP BY inventory_year, state_code_name
),
ranked_subplot AS (
  SELECT
    'subplot' AS plot_type,
    inventory_year,
    state,
    avg_size,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY inventory_year ORDER BY avg_size DESC) AS rn
  FROM avg_subplot
),
ranked_macroplot AS (
  SELECT
    'macroplot' AS plot_type,
    inventory_year,
    state,
    avg_size,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY inventory_year ORDER BY avg_size DESC) AS rn
  FROM avg_macroplot
)
SELECT plot_type, inventory_year, state, avg_size
FROM ranked_subplot
WHERE rn = 1
UNION ALL
SELECT plot_type, inventory_year, state, avg_size
FROM ranked_macroplot
WHERE rn = 1
ORDER BY inventory_year, plot_type

records.question: Can you provide a comprehensive list of all ports in region number 6585 that lie within U.S. state boundaries and have been affected by named storms in the North Atlantic basin with wind speeds of at least 35 knots and a Saffir-Simpson classification of at least minimal tropical storm strength, including for each port its name, the state name, the distinct years in which storms occurred, the total count of distinct storms, the distinct storm names, the average storm category, the average wind speed, and the respective geometries for both the port and the tropical storm areas?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
    wpi.port_name,
    s.state_name,
    h.season AS storm_year,
    COUNT(DISTINCT h.sid) AS total_distinct_storms,
    LISTAGG(DISTINCT h.name, ', ') AS distinct_storm_names,
    AVG(h.usa_sshs) AS avg_storm_category,
    AVG(h.usa_wind) AS avg_wind_speed,
    ST_MAKEPOINT(wpi.port_longitude, wpi.port_latitude) AS port_geometry,
    ST_MAKELINE(
        ST_MAKEPOINT(wpi.port_longitude, wpi.port_latitude),
        ST_MAKEPOINT(h.longitude, h.latitude)
    ) AS storm_area_geometry
FROM
    NOAA_PORTS.GEO_INTERNATIONAL_PORTS.WORLD_PORT_INDEX wpi
    JOIN NOAA_PORTS.GEO_US_BOUNDARIES.STATES s
        ON ST_WITHIN(
            ST_MAKEPOINT(wpi.port_longitude, wpi.port_latitude),
            s.state_geom
        )
    JOIN NOAA_PORTS.NOAA_HURRICANES.HURRICANES h
        ON h.basin = 'NA'
        AND h.wmo_wind >= 35
        AND h.usa_sshs >= 1
        AND ST_WITHIN(
            ST_MAKEPOINT(wpi.port_longitude, wpi.port_latitude),
            ST_MAKEPOINT(h.longitude, h.latitude)
        )
WHERE
    wpi.region_number = '6585'
GROUP BY
    wpi.port_name,
    s.state_name,
    h.season,
    wpi.port_longitude,
    wpi.port_latitude,
    h.longitude,
    h.latitude
ORDER BY
    wpi.port_name,
    h.season

records.question: Which single port, listed under region number '6585', is located within a U.S. state boundary and appears most frequently inside the geographic areas of named tropical storms with wind speeds of at least 35 knots in the North Atlantic basin, excluding those labeled 'NOT_NAMED'?
records.pred_sql:
WITH named_storms AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT sid, name, iso_time, latitude, longitude
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.noaa_hurricanes.hurricanes`
  WHERE basin = 'NA'
    AND wmo_wind >= 35
    AND name != 'NOT_NAMED'
),
storm_points AS (
  SELECT sid, name, ST_GEOGPOINT(longitude, latitude) AS point_geom
  FROM named_storms
),
port_candidates AS (
  SELECT index_number, port_name, port_latitude, port_longitude,
         ST_GEOGPOINT(port_longitude, port_latitude) AS port_geom
  FROM `bigquery-public-data.geo_international_ports.world_port_index`
  WHERE region_number = '6585'
),
ports_in_us AS (
  SELECT p.index_number, p.port_name, p.port_geom
  FROM port_candidates p
  JOIN `bigquery-public-data.geo_us_boundaries.states` s
    ON ST_WITHIN(p.port_geom, s.state_geom)
),
ports_in_storms AS (
  SELECT p.index_number, p.port_name, sp.sid
  FROM ports_in_us p
  JOIN storm_points sp
    ON ST_WITHIN(p.port_geom, sp.point_geom)
)
SELECT port_name
FROM ports_in_storms
GROUP BY port_name
ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
LIMIT 1
