records.question: Generate a daily detailed sales report for each product under the 'Manufacturing' distributor view, covering the 30 days leading up to February 6, 2022, by joining the sales, traffic, inventory, and net PPM data on date, ASIN, program, period, and distributor_view. The report must include total ordered units, ordered revenue, average selling price, glance views, conversion rate, shipped units, shipped revenue, average net PPM, average procurable product OOS, total on-hand units and value, net received units and value, open purchase order quantities, unfilled customer ordered units, and average vendor confirmation rate, receive fill rate, sell-through rate, and vendor lead time.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
    s.DATE,
    s.ASIN,
    s.PROGRAM,
    s.PERIOD,
    s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW,
    s.PRODUCT_TITLE,
    SUM(s.ORDERED_UNITS) AS total_ordered_units,
    SUM(s.ORDERED_REVENUE) AS total_ordered_revenue,
    CASE WHEN SUM(s.ORDERED_UNITS) > 0 THEN SUM(s.ORDERED_REVENUE) / SUM(s.ORDERED_UNITS) ELSE NULL END AS avg_selling_price,
    SUM(t.GLANCE_VIEWS) AS total_glance_views,
    CASE WHEN SUM(t.GLANCE_VIEWS) > 0 THEN SUM(s.ORDERED_UNITS) / SUM(t.GLANCE_VIEWS) ELSE NULL END AS conversion_rate,
    SUM(s.SHIPPED_UNITS) AS total_shipped_units,
    SUM(s.SHIPPED_REVENUE) AS total_shipped_revenue,
    AVG(n.NET_PPM) AS avg_net_ppm,
    AVG(i.PROCURABLE_PRODUCT_OOS) AS avg_procurable_product_oos,
    SUM(i.SELLABLE_ON_HAND_UNITS) AS total_on_hand_units,
    SUM(i.SELLABLE_ON_HAND_INVENTORY) AS total_on_hand_value,
    SUM(i.NET_RECEIVED_UNITS) AS total_net_received_units,
    SUM(i.NET_RECEIVED) AS total_net_received_value,
    SUM(i.OPEN_PURCHASE_ORDER_QUANTITY) AS total_open_purchase_order_qty,
    SUM(i.UNFILLED_CUSTOMER_ORDERED_UNITS) AS total_unfilled_customer_ordered_units,
    AVG(i.VENDOR_CONFIRMATION_RATE) AS avg_vendor_confirmation_rate,
    AVG(i.RECEIVE_FILL_RATE) AS avg_receive_fill_rate,
    AVG(i.SELL_THROUGH_RATE) AS avg_sell_through_rate,
    AVG(i.OVERALL_VENDOR_LEAD_TIME_DAYS) AS avg_vendor_lead_time_days
FROM
    AMAZON_VENDOR_ANALYTICS__SAMPLE_DATASET.PUBLIC.RETAIL_ANALYTICS_SALES s
    LEFT JOIN AMAZON_VENDOR_ANALYTICS__SAMPLE_DATASET.PUBLIC.RETAIL_ANALYTICS_TRAFFIC t
        ON s.DATE = t.DATE
        AND s.ASIN = t.ASIN
        AND s.PROGRAM = t.PROGRAM
        AND s.PERIOD = t.PERIOD
        AND s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW = t.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW
    LEFT JOIN AMAZON_VENDOR_ANALYTICS__SAMPLE_DATASET.PUBLIC.RETAIL_ANALYTICS_INVENTORY i
        ON s.DATE = i.DATE
        AND s.ASIN = i.ASIN
        AND s.PROGRAM = i.PROGRAM
        AND s.PERIOD = i.PERIOD
        AND s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW = i.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW
    LEFT JOIN AMAZON_VENDOR_ANALYTICS__SAMPLE_DATASET.PUBLIC.RETAIL_ANALYTICS_NET_PPM n
        ON s.DATE = n.DATE
        AND s.ASIN = n.ASIN
        AND s.PROGRAM = n.PROGRAM
        AND s.PERIOD = n.PERIOD
        AND s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW = n.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW
WHERE
    s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW = 'Manufacturing'
    AND s.DATE >= DATEADD(DAY, -30, '2022-02-06')
    AND s.DATE < '2022-02-06'
GROUP BY
    s.DATE,
    s.ASIN,
    s.PROGRAM,
    s.PERIOD,
    s.DISTRIBUTOR_VIEW,
    s.PRODUCT_TITLE
ORDER BY
    s.DATE,
    s.ASIN

records.question: Find the top 10 northernmost addresses in Florida's largest zip code area. What are their address numbers, street names, and types?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    a.NUMBER,
    a.STREET,
    a.STREET_TYPE
FROM US_ADDRESSES__POI.CYBERSYN.US_ADDRESSES a
JOIN (
    SELECT ZIP
    FROM US_ADDRESSES__POI.CYBERSYN.US_ADDRESSES
    WHERE STATE = 'FL'
    GROUP BY ZIP
    ORDER BY COUNT(*) DESC
    LIMIT 1
) z ON a.ZIP = z.ZIP
WHERE a.STATE = 'FL'
ORDER BY a.LATITUDE DESC
LIMIT 10

records.question: A real estate company needs a detailed side-by-side comparison of buildings in Amsterdam and Rotterdam. They require a report showing each building class and subclass, with the total surface area (in square meters) and the total number of buildings for each classification category in both cities. The data should be organized by building class and subclass in ascending order, with Amsterdam and Rotterdam statistics presented in parallel columns to facilitate direct comparison. Can you generate this comprehensive building classification comparison report?
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
    b.CLASS,
    b.SUBCLASS,
    SUM(CASE WHEN b.BUILDING_LOCATION = 'Amsterdam' THEN TRY_CAST(b.SURFACE_AREA_SQ_M AS FLOAT) ELSE 0 END) AS AMSTERDAM_SURFACE_AREA_SQ_M,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN b.BUILDING_LOCATION = 'Amsterdam' THEN 1 END) AS AMSTERDAM_BUILDING_COUNT,
    SUM(CASE WHEN b.BUILDING_LOCATION = 'Rotterdam' THEN TRY_CAST(b.SURFACE_AREA_SQ_M AS FLOAT) ELSE 0 END) AS ROTTERDAM_SURFACE_AREA_SQ_M,
    COUNT(CASE WHEN b.BUILDING_LOCATION = 'Rotterdam' THEN 1 END) AS ROTTERDAM_BUILDING_COUNT
FROM NETHERLANDS_OPEN_MAP_DATA.NETHERLANDS.V_BUILDING b
WHERE b.BUILDING_LOCATION IN ('Amsterdam', 'Rotterdam')
GROUP BY b.CLASS, b.SUBCLASS
ORDER BY b.CLASS ASC, b.SUBCLASS ASC

records.question: Produce a report for ERCOT on October 1, 2022, that combines hourly data on day-ahead and real-time prices from node ID 10000697078, load forecasts (datatypeid 19060) and actual loads, plus wind (forecast datatypeid 9285, actual datatypeid 16) and solar (forecast datatypeid 662, actual datatypeid 650) generation forecasts and actuals from object ID 10000712973. This report should include time zone alignments, peak classifications, and net load calculations, providing insights into daily operational dynamics and efficiency.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT
    imt.DATETIME,
    imt.TIMEZONE,
    imt.DATETIME_UTC,
    imt.ONPEAK,
    imt.OFFPEAK,
    imt.WEPEAK,
    imt.WDPEAK,
    imt.MARKETDAY,
    dp.DALMP,
    dp.RTLMP,
    lf.VALUE AS load_forecast,
    rl.LOAD AS rtload,
    wf.VALUE AS wind_gen_forecast,
    wa.VALUE AS wind_gen,
    sf.VALUE AS solar_gen_forecast,
    sa.VALUE AS solar_gen,
    (lf.VALUE - (COALESCE(wf.VALUE, 0) + COALESCE(sf.VALUE, 0))) AS net_load_forecast,
    (rl.LOAD - (COALESCE(wa.VALUE, 0) + COALESCE(sa.VALUE, 0))) AS net_load_real_time
FROM YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.ISO_MARKET_TIMES_SAMPLE imt
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.DART_PRICES_SAMPLE dp
    ON imt.DATETIME = dp.DATETIME
    AND imt.ISO = dp.ISO
    AND dp.PROXYCODE = '10000697078'
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE lf
    ON imt.DATETIME = lf.DATETIME
    AND lf.DATATYPEID = 19060
    AND lf.LOADID = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.RT_LOADS_SAMPLE rl
    ON imt.DATETIME = rl.DATETIME
    AND rl.LOADID = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE wf
    ON imt.DATETIME = wf.DATETIME
    AND wf.DATATYPEID = 9285
    AND wf.LOADID = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE wa
    ON imt.DATETIME = wa.DATETIME
    AND wa.DATATYPEID = 16
    AND wa.LOADID = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE sf
    ON imt.DATETIME = sf.DATETIME
    AND sf.DATATYPEID = 662
    AND sf.LOADID = 10000712973
LEFT JOIN YES_ENERGY__SAMPLE_DATA.yes_energy_sample.TS_FORECAST_SAMPLE sa
    ON imt.DATETIME = sa.DATETIME
    AND sa.DATATYPEID = 650
    AND sa.LOADID = 10000712973
WHERE imt.ISO = 'ERCOT'
  AND imt.MARKETDAY = '2022-10-01'
ORDER BY imt.DATETIME

records.question: Determine the population distribution within each block group relative to its census tract in New York State using 2021 ACS data. Include block group ID, census value, state county tract ID, total tract population, and the population ratio of each block group.

    dg.BlockGroupID,
    fc.CensusValue,
    dg.StateCountyTractID,
    SUM(fc.CensusValue) OVER (PARTITION BY dg.StateCountyTractID) AS TotalTractPopulation,
    fc.CensusValue / NULLIF(SUM(fc.CensusValue) OVER (PARTITION BY dg.StateCountyTractID), 0) AS PopulationRatio
FROM CENSUS_GALAXY__ZIP_CODE_TO_BLOCK_GROUP_SAMPLE.public.Fact_CensusValues_ACS2021 fc

    ON fc.MetricID = dm.MetricID
WHERE dm.MetricID = 'B01001_001E'  -- Total population metric
  AND dg.StateAbbrev = 'NY'

records.question: What is the New York State ZIP code with the highest number of commuters traveling over one hour, according to 2021 ACS data? Include the zip code, the total commuters, state benchmark for this duration, and state population.
records.pred_sql:
SELECT 
    f.ZipCode,
    f.CensusValueByZip AS total_commuters,
    s.StateBenchmarkValue AS state_benchmark,
    s.TotalStatePopulation AS state_population
FROM CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Fact_CensusValues_ACS2021_ByZip f
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Dim_CensusMetrics m
    ON f.MetricID = m.MetricID
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.Fact_StateBenchmark_ACS2021 s
    ON f.MetricID = s.MetricID AND s.StateAbbrev = 'NY'
JOIN CENSUS_GALAXY__AIML_MODEL_DATA_ENRICHMENT_SAMPLE.public.LU_GeographyExpanded g
    ON f.ZipCode = g.ZipCode AND g.PreferredStateAbbrev = 'NY'
WHERE m.MetricTableID = 'S0802'
  AND m.Descriptor1 = 'COMMUTING TIME'
  AND m.Descriptor2 = '60+ minutes'
ORDER BY f.CensusValueByZip DESC
LIMIT 1
